title: Implement connection lifecycle state machine and protocol versioning
purpose: Implements the connection lifecycle state machine that enforces valid frame sequences and the protocol version negotiation that happens during the Connect/ConnectAck handshake. The state machine prevents out-of-sequence frames (no Publish before authentication, no Subscribe before Connect) and the version negotiation selects the highest mutually supported version or rejects incompatible connections. This is what turns a raw TCP byte stream into a protocol-aware connection.
task: Build the connection lifecycle state machine, the handshake exchange, and protocol versioning. Define ConnectionState (Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, Closed) with valid frame transitions per state. Define ProtocolVersion with major/minor components and version range negotiation. Implement the Connect/ConnectAck exchange: client sends supported version range and auth token, server selects the highest mutually supported version and responds with capabilities. Enforce Ping/Pong only in Active state on stream 0. Reject frames invalid for the current state with ProtocolError carrying the appropriate reason code. Scope boundary: this brief handles connection-level state and versioning. It does not implement multiplexing (PROTO-004), backpressure state (PROTO-005), or schema negotiation (PROTO-006).

requirements.title: Define ProtocolVersion with major and minor components
requirements.spec: ProtocolVersion struct SHALL contain major (u16) and minor (u16) version fields. ProtocolVersion SHALL implement Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, and Ord (ordered by major then minor). ProtocolVersion SHALL serialize to 4 bytes (big-endian u16 + u16) for wire transport.
acceptance: ProtocolVersion has fields: major (u16), minor (u16).
ProtocolVersion(1, 0) < ProtocolVersion(1, 1) < ProtocolVersion(2, 0).
ProtocolVersion serializes to exactly 4 bytes.
ProtocolVersion round-trips through serialize/deserialize.
ProtocolVersion implements Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:1 — existing placeholder; implement ProtocolVersion here rather than creating a new path.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:5-7,64-73,150-163 — existing codec serializes numeric fields with big-endian to_be_bytes/from_be_bytes and returns ProtocolError instead of panicking.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs:1-15 — module root is declarations and re-exports only; add a pub use for ProtocolVersion here if it should be part of protocol::*.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:311-320 — representative derive-based Ord implementation; field order is used for ordering.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lints deny unwrap, expect, panic, and unsafe code.
context: ProtocolVersion should be a public struct with fields ordered as major then minor so derived PartialOrd/Ord naturally sorts by major, then minor.

The protocol codec helpers are private to the codec module, so version.rs should expose its own small wire helpers, for example WIRE_LEN = 4 plus to_wire_bytes/from_wire_bytes or serialize/deserialize.
Deserialize from a slice/array without unwrap or expect; malformed length should become ProtocolError::CodecError via ProtocolError::codec.
Tests in this crate typically return Result<(), ProtocolError> and use ? rather than unwrap, matching the workspace clippy policy.
scout.approach: Define #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)] pub struct ProtocolVersion { pub major: u16, pub minor: u16 }. Add a const WIRE_LEN: usize = 4, a const new constructor, a method that returns [u8; 4] as major.to_be_bytes() followed by minor.to_be_bytes(), and a fallible deserialize/from_wire_bytes that checks the input length and uses u16::from_be_bytes. Add unit tests in version.rs for ordering, exact 4-byte serialization, round-trip, and trait usability; only touch mod.rs to re-export the type if callers/tests need protocol::ProtocolVersion.
scout.notes: R4/R5 will need to migrate Frame::Connect and Frame::ConnectAck from the current u16 version fields to this new type; R1 can be implemented independently, but the codec will not use it until those later changes land.

files_changed.note: Implemented ProtocolVersion with major/minor fields, derived ordering traits, 4-byte big-endian wire helpers, and unit tests.
Re-exported ProtocolVersion from the protocol module root.

dev.how: Added a public ProtocolVersion struct with major then minor u16 fields so derived Ord naturally sorts by major then minor. Added WIRE_LEN, const constructor, serialize/to_wire_bytes, deserialize/from_wire_bytes that reject wrong-length input with ProtocolError::CodecError, and tests for trait bounds, ordering, exact wire length, and round-trip behavior.

checklist.note: ProtocolVersion defines public major and minor u16 components and serializes to exactly four wire bytes.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolVersion has fields: major (u16), minor (u16).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:5-10 defines public `ProtocolVersion { pub major: u16, pub minor: u16 }`; test `protocol::version::tests::version_trait_bounds_are_available` passed under `cargo test`.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolVersion(1, 0) < ProtocolVersion(1, 1) < ProtocolVersion(2, 0).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:4 derives `PartialOrd, Ord` with `major` before `minor`; `protocol::version::tests::versions_order_by_major_then_minor` at version.rs:98-102 asserts both ordering comparisons and passed.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolVersion serializes to exactly 4 bytes.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:13-28 sets `WIRE_LEN = 4` and serializes two big-endian u16 values; `protocol::version::tests::version_serializes_to_exactly_four_bytes` at version.rs:104-110 passed.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolVersion round-trips through serialize/deserialize.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:24-60 implements `serialize`, `deserialize`, and `from_wire_bytes`; `protocol::version::tests::version_round_trips_through_wire_bytes` at version.rs:112-120 passed.

acceptance.criterion: ProtocolVersion implements Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:4 derives `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord`; compile-time assertion test at version.rs:91-96 passed.

requirements.title: Implement version negotiation selecting highest mutual version
requirements.spec: WHEN the client sends a Connect frame with a version range (min_version, max_version), THE SYSTEM SHALL select the highest version from the server's supported set that falls within the client's range. IF no version in the server's set falls within the client's range, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a ConnectError frame with a version-mismatch reason code. The server's supported versions SHALL be configurable, not hardcoded.
acceptance: Client range [1.0, 2.0] with server supporting {1.0, 1.1, 2.0, 3.0} selects 2.0.
Client range [1.0, 1.5] with server supporting {2.0, 3.0} returns ConnectError with version-mismatch.
Client range [1.0, 1.0] with server supporting {1.0} selects 1.0.
The server's supported version set is passed as a parameter, not hardcoded.
negotiate_version returns Result<ProtocolVersion, ProtocolError>.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:1 — negotiate_version should live next to ProtocolVersion and take the server-supported set as an argument.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:37-40,63-80,86-99 — VersionMismatch already exists with a stable VERSION_MISMATCH_CODE and reason_code/message helpers.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:153-160 — ConnectAck/ConnectError are the existing handshake success/failure frame variants; ConnectError carries reason_code.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:6-14,20-23 — design requires highest mutual version, additive-only versioning, and clear rejection for incompatible connections.
docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-003.json:33-49 — exact R2 acceptance cases and checklist references.
context: The supported server versions must be supplied to negotiate_version; do not introduce a production hardcoded SUPPORTED_VERSIONS list.
Because ProtocolVersion derives Ord, the highest mutual version can be selected with an inclusive range filter followed by max().
The Result error should be ProtocolError::VersionMismatch; converting that into a ConnectError frame should use ProtocolError::VERSION_MISMATCH_CODE or error.reason_code().
An empty supported set or an inverted client range naturally produces VersionMismatch and should not panic.
scout.approach: Add pub fn negotiate_version(min_version: ProtocolVersion, max_version: ProtocolVersion, supported_versions: &[ProtocolVersion]) -> Result<ProtocolVersion, ProtocolError>. Iterate over supported_versions.copied(), filter versions where min_version <= version && version <= max_version, take max(), and return Err(ProtocolError::VersionMismatch { message: Some(...) }) when no candidate exists. Cover the three acceptance cases with tests using local arrays, and, if implementing a handshake helper for error responses, construct Frame::ConnectError with the version-mismatch reason code rather than duplicating numeric literals.
scout.notes: The spec says the system returns a ConnectError frame, while the acceptance says negotiate_version returns Result<ProtocolVersion, ProtocolError>. The clean split is: version.rs returns the error, handshake.rs can turn that error into a ConnectError frame when needed.

files_changed.note: Added negotiate_version with configurable supported-version slice and acceptance tests.
Added connect_error_frame helper that maps ProtocolError reason codes, including version mismatch, into ConnectError frames.
Re-exported negotiate_version.

dev.how: Implemented negotiate_version(min_version, max_version, supported_versions) -> Result<ProtocolVersion, ProtocolError> using the caller-supplied supported slice, filtering inclusively over the client's range and selecting max(). No hardcoded server versions were introduced. Incompatible ranges return ProtocolError::VersionMismatch, and the handshake helper can convert that error into a ConnectError frame using the stable version-mismatch reason code.

checklist.note: Highest mutual version selection is covered for [1.0, 2.0] over {1.0, 1.1, 2.0, 3.0}, selecting 2.0.
No mutual version returns ProtocolError::VersionMismatch, and connect_error_frame emits a ConnectError with error.reason_code(), including VERSION_MISMATCH_CODE.

stories.note: The protocol now provides automatic version negotiation from a client range and server-supported set.

acceptance.criterion: Client range [1.0, 2.0] with server supporting {1.0, 1.1, 2.0, 3.0} selects 2.0.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:69-82 filters the supplied set inclusively and selects `.max()`; `protocol::version::tests::negotiation_selects_highest_mutual_version` at version.rs:130-147 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Client range [1.0, 1.5] with server supporting {2.0, 3.0} returns ConnectError with version-mismatch.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:79-81 returns `ProtocolError::VersionMismatch` when no mutual version exists; crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:51-57 maps any `ProtocolError` to `Frame::ConnectError` using `error.reason_code()`, and `protocol::handshake::tests::connect_error_frame_uses_error_reason_code_and_message` at handshake.rs:148-165 verifies `VERSION_MISMATCH_CODE`.

acceptance.criterion: Client range [1.0, 1.0] with server supporting {1.0} selects 1.0.
acceptance.evidence: `protocol::version::tests::negotiation_selects_exact_single_version` at crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:162-174 exercises the exact-range case and passed.

acceptance.criterion: The server's supported version set is passed as a parameter, not hardcoded.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:69-73 defines `negotiate_version(min_version, max_version, supported_versions: &[ProtocolVersion])`; selection only iterates that parameter at version.rs:74-78.

acceptance.criterion: negotiate_version returns Result<ProtocolVersion, ProtocolError>.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/version.rs:69-73 declares `pub fn negotiate_version(...) -> Result<ProtocolVersion, ProtocolError>`; it compiled under `cargo check`.

requirements.title: Define ConnectionState enum and state transition rules
requirements.spec: ConnectionState enum SHALL have variants: Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, Closed. The state machine SHALL enforce valid frame sequences per state. WHEN a frame is received that is invalid for the current state, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition with the current state and the received frame type. State transitions: Connecting accepts Connect only; Authenticating accepts ConnectAck/ConnectError only; Active accepts all application frames plus Ping/Pong/Disconnect; Closing accepts Disconnect acknowledgement only; Closed accepts nothing.
acceptance: ConnectionState has exactly 5 variants: Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, Closed.
Receiving Publish in Connecting state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition.
Receiving Subscribe in Active state is valid (no error).
Receiving Ping in Connecting state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition.
The error carries both the current state and the rejected frame type.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:1 — existing placeholder for ConnectionState and transition APIs.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:7-67,301-383 — FrameType categories plus frame_type(), flags(), and stream_id() accessors the state machine should use.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:29-31,70-99 — InvalidStateTransition currently carries only message and must be extended to include state and frame type.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:282-299 — decode validates stream placement and returns Unknown frames without error before lifecycle sees frames.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:33-45,54-58 — protocol module structure plus constraints around stream 0, unknown frame skipping, and explicit errors.
context: ConnectionState must be public and derive at least Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq so it can be copied through transition logic and stored in ProtocolError.
ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition needs new fields such as current_state: ConnectionState and frame_type: FrameType; update reason_code() and message() matches accordingly.
Active should accept all known non-control/application frame types plus Ping, Pong, and Disconnect; Connect/ConnectAck/ConnectError should not be accepted once Active.
Unknown frame types are a protocol-versioning feature, not a lifecycle violation; preserve CN9 by skipping/ignoring Unknown frames or ensuring they bypass disconnect-producing state rejection.
scout.approach: Implement ConnectionState { Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, Closed } and a small table-driven API such as validate_frame_type(self, frame_type: FrameType) -> Result<(), ProtocolError> plus transition(self, frame_type: FrameType) -> Result<Self, ProtocolError>. Connecting accepts only Connect and moves to Authenticating; Authenticating accepts ConnectAck/ConnectError, with ConnectAck moving to Active and ConnectError likely closing the connection; Active accepts non-control known frames plus Ping/Pong/Disconnect, with Disconnect moving to Closing; Closing accepts Disconnect as the acknowledgement and moves to Closed; Closed rejects everything. Use a helper constructor for invalid-state errors so every rejection includes both current_state and frame_type.
scout.notes: There is no distinct DisconnectAck frame type in FrameType, so implementers need to treat a second Disconnect in Closing as the acknowledgement and document that choice in tests. Also decide explicitly whether ConnectError transitions to Closed or Closing; the brief only asserts ConnectAck -> Active.

files_changed.note: Implemented ConnectionState, transition validation, lifecycle handling result, and state-machine tests.
Extended ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition to carry current_state and frame_type, with reason/message helpers updated.

dev.how: Added ConnectionState with exactly Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, and Closed. The lifecycle API validates frame types, transitions accepted frames, and rejects invalid sequences with ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition containing both the current state and rejected FrameType. Connecting accepts only Connect, Authenticating accepts ConnectAck/ConnectError, Active accepts application frames plus Ping/Pong/Disconnect while rejecting handshake frames, Closing treats Disconnect as the acknowledgement, and Closed rejects all frames.
dev.deviation: Clarified unknown-frame handling by allowing Unknown frame types to bypass lifecycle rejection only while the connection is not Closed, preserving forward-compatible skipping without violating the requirement that Closed accepts nothing.

checklist.note: ConnectionState defines the five required states.
Invalid frame/state combinations return ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition with the stable invalid-state reason code.

stories.note: Conversation lifecycle frame types are accepted in Active state, allowing open/message/close/error frames to flow in order after handshake.
Out-of-sequence frames such as Publish or Ping during Connecting are rejected with explicit state-transition errors.

acceptance.criterion: ConnectionState has exactly 5 variants: Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, Closed.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:4-16 defines exactly `Connecting`, `Authenticating`, `Active`, `Closing`, and `Closed`.

acceptance.criterion: Receiving Publish in Connecting state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:90-92 accepts only `Connect` in `Connecting`; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::connecting_rejects_publish_with_state_and_frame_type` at lifecycle.rs:150-162 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Receiving Subscribe in Active state is valid (no error).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:101-121 includes `FrameType::Subscribe` in Active accepted frames; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::active_accepts_subscribe` at lifecycle.rs:164-167 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Receiving Ping in Connecting state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:90-92 excludes Ping in `Connecting`; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::connecting_rejects_ping_with_state_and_frame_type` at lifecycle.rs:169-181 and `ping_in_connecting_returns_invalid_state_transition` at lifecycle.rs:259-272 passed.

acceptance.criterion: The error carries both the current state and the rejected frame type.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:31-35 defines `InvalidStateTransition { current_state, frame_type, message }`; lifecycle tests at lifecycle.rs:154-160 and 264-270 match both fields.

requirements.title: Implement Connect frame with version range and auth token
requirements.spec: The Connect frame payload SHALL carry: min_version (ProtocolVersion), max_version (ProtocolVersion), and auth_token (Vec<u8> — opaque, per P6). The handshake module SHALL provide a function to construct a Connect frame on stream 0. The auth token is opaque — the protocol carries it without interpretation.
acceptance: Connect frame carries min_version, max_version, and auth_token fields.
Connect frame is always on stream 0.
auth_token is stored as opaque bytes — no validation or interpretation.
Connect frame round-trips through the PROTO-001 codec.
Constructing a Connect frame with stream_id != 0 returns an error.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:1 — existing placeholder for Connect constructor helpers.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:143-160,357-413 — Connect currently stores min_version/max_version as u16, control frame stream_id() is hardcoded to 0, and validate_stream enforces stream invariants.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:134-146,182-195 — Connect payload length/encoding currently writes two u16 version fields plus auth token.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/known.rs:48-63 — Connect decoding currently reads two u16 values for versions.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/tests.rs:193-224 — sample control frames drive all-frame codec round-trip coverage and must be updated.
context: The Connect variant already exists but does not match this brief: min_version and max_version need to become ProtocolVersion, each serialized as 4 bytes.

Frame::Connect cannot represent a non-zero stream id because control frame stream_id() always returns 0; the handshake constructor must accept a stream_id parameter and validate it before constructing the enum.
auth_token should remain Vec<u8> and opaque; do not parse UTF-8, validate JWTs, or interpret token contents.
Round-trip acceptance depends on codec changes, not only handshake.rs, because decode/encode currently use the old u16 layout.
scout.approach: In handshake.rs, add a public constructor such as connect_frame(stream_id: u32, min_version: ProtocolVersion, max_version: ProtocolVersion, auth_token: impl Into<Vec<u8>>) -> Result<Frame, ProtocolError>. Call validate_stream(FrameType::Connect, stream_id) and return Frame::Connect { flags: 0, min_version, max_version, auth_token: auth_token.into() }. Update Frame::Connect field types, codec payload length, writer, known-frame decoder, and codec sample frames so the wire format is min ProtocolVersion (4 bytes), max ProtocolVersion (4 bytes), then the existing length-prefixed auth bytes.
scout.notes: frame.rs is already 467 lines, so avoid adding substantial tests there; put constructor tests in handshake.rs and codec round-trip tests in codec/tests.rs. The brief lists only handshake.rs for R4, but the codec cannot satisfy acceptance without the frame/codec edits above.

files_changed.note: Added connect_frame constructor with stream-0 validation and opaque auth-token tests.
Changed Frame::Connect to carry ProtocolVersion min_version/max_version and Vec<u8> auth_token.
Updated Connect payload length and encoding to write two 4-byte ProtocolVersion values followed by length-prefixed auth bytes.
Updated Connect decoding to read ProtocolVersion values.
Updated all-frame round-trip fixtures for the new Connect payload shape.
Re-exported validate_stream used by handshake helpers.

dev.how: The Connect frame now carries min_version and max_version as ProtocolVersion plus an opaque Vec<u8> auth_token. The public connect_frame constructor accepts a stream_id parameter, validates that Connect is on stream 0, and stores the token bytes without UTF-8/auth/JWT interpretation. Codec encode/decode now round-trips the new wire layout: min ProtocolVersion, max ProtocolVersion, then the existing length-prefixed auth token.

checklist.note: Connect carries the client's supported version range and opaque auth token and round-trips through the codec.

stories.note: The client can initiate handshake with a version range used by negotiation.

acceptance.criterion: Connect frame carries min_version, max_version, and auth_token fields.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:146-152 defines `Frame::Connect { min_version, max_version, auth_token }`; handshake constructor populates them at handshake.rs:11-24.

acceptance.criterion: Connect frame is always on stream 0.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:11-18 validates `FrameType::Connect` through `validate_stream`; `Frame::stream_id()` returns 0 for Connect at frame.rs:365-370; `connect_constructor_builds_stream_zero_frame_with_opaque_token` at handshake.rs:65-89 passed.

acceptance.criterion: auth_token is stored as opaque bytes — no validation or interpretation.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:15-22 converts the token directly into `Vec<u8>`; codec writes/reads it as a bytes field at codec.rs:190-199 and codec/known.rs:58-63. Test at handshake.rs:66-87 uses non-UTF-8 bytes including 0xff and passed.

acceptance.criterion: Connect frame round-trips through the PROTO-001 codec.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:137-141 and 190-199 encode Connect; codec/known.rs:58-63 decodes Connect; `protocol::handshake::tests::connect_frame_round_trips_through_codec` at handshake.rs:103-114 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Constructing a Connect frame with stream_id != 0 returns an error.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:17 calls `validate_stream`; `protocol::handshake::tests::connect_constructor_rejects_non_zero_stream` at handshake.rs:91-101 passed with `ProtocolError::InvalidStream`.

requirements.title: Implement ConnectAck frame with selected version and capabilities
requirements.spec: The ConnectAck frame payload SHALL carry: selected_version (ProtocolVersion) and capabilities (a bitfield u32 representing server feature flags). The handshake module SHALL provide a function to construct a ConnectAck frame on stream 0 after successful version negotiation and authentication.
acceptance: ConnectAck frame carries selected_version and capabilities fields.
ConnectAck frame is always on stream 0.
capabilities is a u32 bitfield (individual flag meanings are defined by the server, not this brief).
ConnectAck round-trips through the PROTO-001 codec.
Receiving ConnectAck transitions the connection state from Authenticating to Active.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:1 — add ConnectAck constructor helper alongside the Connect helper.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:153-155,301-366 — ConnectAck currently carries only version: u16 and is treated as a stream-0 control frame.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:134-146,182-195 — ConnectAck encoded length and payload writing currently account for only one u16.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/known.rs:60-63 — ConnectAck decoder currently reads only version.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:1 — Authenticating + ConnectAck -> Active transition belongs in the lifecycle state machine.
context: ConnectAck should be reshaped to carry selected_version: ProtocolVersion and capabilities: u32; the exact field name selected_version matters for acceptance readability.
capabilities is a raw u32 bitfield for the server to populate; do not define or validate individual feature flags in this brief.
The constructor should validate stream 0 even though the Frame enum stores control streams implicitly.
Lifecycle tests should prove receiving ConnectAck in Authenticating transitions to Active; codec tests should separately prove capabilities survive round-trip.
scout.approach: Change Frame::ConnectAck to { flags: u8, selected_version: ProtocolVersion, capabilities: u32 } and update frame_type/flags/stream accessors as needed. In handshake.rs, add connect_ack_frame(stream_id, selected_version, capabilities) -> Result<Frame, ProtocolError> that calls validate_stream(FrameType::ConnectAck, stream_id). Update encoded_payload_len to ProtocolVersion::WIRE_LEN + U32_LEN, write/read selected_version as two big-endian u16s and capabilities as big-endian u32, refresh codec sample frames, and add the lifecycle transition test for Authenticating -> Active.
scout.notes: This is another requirement whose file list understates the necessary codec work. If the old ConnectAck { version: u16 } remains, the PROTO-001 round-trip acceptance for selected_version + capabilities cannot pass.

files_changed.note: Added connect_ack_frame constructor with stream-0 validation and codec round-trip tests.
Changed Frame::ConnectAck to carry selected_version: ProtocolVersion and capabilities: u32.
Updated ConnectAck payload length and encoding for ProtocolVersion plus u32 capabilities.
Updated ConnectAck decoding to read selected_version and capabilities.
Updated codec fixtures to assert ConnectAck capability bitfield survival.
Added Authenticating + ConnectAck -> Active transition and test coverage.

dev.how: ConnectAck now exposes selected_version and raw u32 capabilities without defining capability meanings. The handshake constructor validates stream 0, codec support round-trips the new payload, and lifecycle transition logic moves Authenticating to Active when ConnectAck is received.

checklist.note: ConnectAck carries selected protocol version plus server capabilities bitfield and round-trips through the codec.

stories.note: Successful negotiation can now be acknowledged with the selected version and server capability bitfield.

acceptance.criterion: ConnectAck frame carries selected_version and capabilities fields.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:153-158 defines `Frame::ConnectAck { selected_version, capabilities }`; handshake constructor populates them at handshake.rs:33-44.

acceptance.criterion: ConnectAck frame is always on stream 0.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/handshake.rs:33-39 validates `FrameType::ConnectAck` through `validate_stream`; `protocol::handshake::tests::connect_ack_constructor_rejects_non_zero_stream` at handshake.rs:133-138 passed.

acceptance.criterion: capabilities is a u32 bitfield (individual flag meanings are defined by the server, not this brief).
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:156-157 stores `capabilities: u32`; handshake.rs:28 documents it as an opaque server-defined bitfield, and no capability flag meanings are defined in the diff.

acceptance.criterion: ConnectAck round-trips through the PROTO-001 codec.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:142 and 200-207 encode ConnectAck; codec/known.rs:64-68 decodes it; `protocol::handshake::tests::connect_ack_frame_round_trips_through_codec` at handshake.rs:140-146 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Receiving ConnectAck transitions the connection state from Authenticating to Active.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:124-130 maps `(Authenticating, ConnectAck)` to `Active`; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::connect_ack_transitions_authenticating_to_active` at lifecycle.rs:199-212 passed.

requirements.title: Enforce Ping/Pong constraints
requirements.spec: Ping and Pong frames SHALL be valid only in the Active state. They SHALL travel on stream 0 only. WHEN a Ping is received in Active state on stream 0, THE SYSTEM SHALL respond with a Pong on stream 0. WHEN a Ping is received on a non-zero stream or in a non-Active state, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ProtocolError.
acceptance: Ping on stream 0 in Active state is valid.
Ping on stream 1 in Active state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStream.
Ping in Connecting state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition.
Receiving Ping generates a Pong response on stream 0.
Pong frames carry no payload.

files: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:1 — implement Active-only Ping/Pong validation and Ping response generation here.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:53-67,271-280,357-413 — Ping/Pong are already control frame types; new_ping and validate_stream enforce stream 0.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:134-146,182-219,282-299 — Ping/Pong payloads are encoded as zero length and stream placement is validated during decode.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/tests.rs:75-90,159-164 — existing tests cover no-payload Ping/Pong round-trip and raw invalid-stream Ping decode.
docs/design/protocol/briefs/PROTO-003.json:109-118 — exact R6 acceptance criteria.
context: Ping/Pong frame types and zero-payload codec behavior already exist; preserve this rather than adding payload structs.
A Ping on stream 1 cannot be represented as Frame::Ping because Frame::stream_id() returns 0 for control frames; test that case through raw decode bytes or validate_stream(FrameType::Ping, 1).
Validation order matters: in Active, a non-zero-stream Ping should be InvalidStream, while a stream-0 Ping in Connecting should be InvalidStateTransition.
Receiving Ping in Active should return a Pong on stream 0, likely Frame::Pong { flags: 0 }, with no transport I/O implemented in this brief.
scout.approach: Extend the lifecycle API with a handler that can validate a frame and optionally produce a response, for example handle_frame(state, frame) -> Result<(ConnectionState, Option<Frame>), ProtocolError> or a mutating equivalent. First enforce stream placement for the frame, then enforce the state table. For Active + Ping, return Some(Frame::Pong { flags: 0 }) and keep the state Active; for Active + Pong return no response; for Connecting + Ping return InvalidStateTransition carrying Connecting and Ping. Keep Ping/Pong on stream 0 by relying on validate_stream and the existing control-frame stream_id behavior.
scout.notes: Do not implement configurable keepalive timers or dead-connection detection here; R6 only requires protocol-level validation and response frame generation.

files_changed.note: Enforced Active-only Ping/Pong validation and added Ping-to-Pong response generation tests.

dev.how: Lifecycle handling first validates stream placement with validate_stream, then applies state rules. Ping/Pong are accepted only in Active; Ping in Connecting returns InvalidStateTransition, stream-1 Ping returns InvalidStream, and Active stream-0 Ping returns a LifecycleAction with a Pong frame on stream 0. Pong remains a zero-payload frame in the existing codec.

checklist.note: Ping/Pong are stream-0 control frames accepted only in Active, and Active Ping generates a stream-0 Pong response.

stories.note: The protocol-level keepalive frames are validated and a Ping produces a Pong response without adding timer/dead-connection policy.

acceptance.criterion: Ping on stream 0 in Active state is valid.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:101-121 accepts Ping in Active and frame.rs:363-370 places Ping on stream 0; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::ping_on_stream_zero_in_active_is_valid` at lifecycle.rs:241-250 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Ping on stream 1 in Active state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStream.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:401-416 enforces stream 0 for control frames including Ping; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::ping_on_stream_one_in_active_returns_invalid_stream` at lifecycle.rs:252-257 and `protocol::codec::tests::decode_rejects_invalid_stream_without_panicking` at codec/tests.rs:159-164 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Ping in Connecting state returns ProtocolError::InvalidStateTransition.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:90-92 accepts only Connect in Connecting; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::ping_in_connecting_returns_invalid_state_transition` at lifecycle.rs:259-272 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Receiving Ping generates a Pong response on stream 0.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:67-78 returns `Some(Frame::Pong { flags: 0 })` for Active Ping; `protocol::lifecycle::tests::ping_generates_pong_response_on_stream_zero` at lifecycle.rs:274-283 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Pong frames carry no payload.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:149 and 241 encode Pong as zero payload; `protocol::codec::tests::no_payload_frames_round_trip_as_header_only` at codec/tests.rs:75-91 asserts Ping/Pong/Disconnect write exactly the 10-byte header with payload length 0 and passed.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement authentication mechanism validation — the protocol carries tokens, not validates them (P6).
SHALL NOT implement stream multiplexing logic — that is PROTO-004.
SHALL NOT implement backpressure state — that is PROTO-005.
SHALL NOT implement schema negotiation — that is PROTO-006.
SHALL NOT define specific capability flag meanings — capabilities is a raw bitfield for the bus to populate.
SHALL NOT hardcode the server's supported protocol versions.
verification: cargo check compiles lifecycle, handshake, and version modules with no errors.
Test: version negotiation selects highest mutual version.
Test: incompatible version range returns ConnectError.
Test: state machine rejects Publish before Active state.
Test: Ping/Pong only valid on stream 0 in Active state.
